title: Daily Briefing — May 28
headline: Tech leads, everything else limps—breadth is getting dangerously narrow.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: Mega-cap tech is still king, with XLK running hot (RS=121.6) and names like MSFT ($425.39, +3.1%) and ARM ($323.76, +7.0%) smashing their SMA20s. SPY and QQQ hugging fresh highs above their mean reversion zones (SMA20: 737.24 for SPY, 705.18 for QQQ), while volatility contracts (VIX 16.15) and Fear & Greed sits at a lukewarm 61.2. That’s not an all-clear: breadth is narrowing, and the rally is getting concentrated.

Sector rotation is mental right now. XLK (tech) is the only leader—everything else (XLE, XLF, XLV, XLI, XLB, XLP, XLU, XLRE) is lagging and carrying 'high' risk. Industrial, staples, materials, and financials are all below their SMA20s or just grinding sideways. If you want momentum, you’re in the tech crowd; for everything else, patience, abundance mindset, don’t chase.

Contrarian signal: Retail is piling into high beta names, but dark pools aren’t showing monster accumulation outside XLK and select tech. XLF ($51.27, bias bear) is flirting with a SMA20 break—financials are the canary in the coal mine (R010). VIX is normal, but this is no time to be complacent; breadth divergence reminds me of late 2021, right before the drawdown. If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Mind your exits: daily closes below 20 MA—especially for lagging ETFs—mean risk gets real fast (R006).
callout_body:
SPY: $750.52 (SMA20: 737.24)—watch for support
MSFT: $425.39 (SMA20: 414.72)—momentum leader
XLK: $184.87 (SMA20: 174.41)—only sector in leading quadrant
XLF: $51.27 (SMA20: 51.54)—financials at risk
NVDA: $213.11 (SMA20: 214.82)—watch for daily close below 20MA
VIX: 16.15—volatility very low, don't sleep on it
Fear & Greed: 61.2—not extreme, but creeping up
action_items: Stick with tech momentum—MSFT, ARM, XLK—while above 20MA; avoid lagging sectors (XLE, XLF, XLI, etc.) unless reversal triggers.
Watch for daily closes below 20 MA on lagging ETFs and risk names (XLF, NVDA, XLE)—if triggered, tighten stops or exit per rule R006.
closing_quote: Breadth narrows, risk rises—patience, react, don’t predict.
